calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order/Entry Of Judgment And Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-01-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JULIANNA AGARDI's Motion To Vacate Order/Entry Of Judgment And Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Julianna Agardi moves under CCP 473(b) "to vacate order/entry of judgment and judgment." However, CCP 473(b) requires the moving party's "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect" and Agardi alleges none of them. The motion is denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2021 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant the City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff Julianna Agardi's first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff asserts that her FAC is filed "with the purpose of enforcing plaintiff's judgment" as to case number CGC-10-500534. (FAC para. 25.) However, in that case, this court granted the City's judgment on the pleadings, dismissed the case with prejudice and entered judgment in favor of the City. The Court of Appeal affirmed this court's judgment. Contrary to plaintiff's apparent misimpression, no judgment was ever entered in her favor. There can be only one final judgment in an action. In the present FAC, plaintiff again alleges the City is violating its obligation to pay General Assistance and is defrauding plaintiff. (FAC paras. 25, 28, 38, 39.) The action is barred by law of the case and/or by res judicata. (Morohoshi v. Pacific Home (2004) 34 Cal.4th 482, 491 ["The decision of an appellate court, stating a rule of law necessary to the decision of the case, conclusively establishes that rule and makes it determinative of the rights of the same parties in any subsequent retrial or appeal in the same case"]; Association of Irritated Residents v. Department of Conservation (2017) 11 Cal.App.3d 517, 530-531 [res judicata prevents the relitigation of the same causes of action between the same parties].) Plaintiff contends that the City's citation to the Court of Appeal's prior opinion is improper. Plaintiff is wrong. (Cal. R. Ct. 8.1115(b)(1) [an unpublished opinion may be cited or relied on when it is relevant under the doctrines of law of the case, res judicata, or collateral estoppel].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JULIANNA AGARDI'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's "motion to strike demurrer" is evidently intended as her opposition to the City's demurrer to the amended complaint above. To the extent that it seeks to strike portions of the City's argument in its moving papers, it is denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
